message:
fix(string_utils): guard out-of-bounds start in find_closing_quote and ASCII-only fast path in find_unquoted_char

Two defensive hardenings on the byte-level scan helpers used by the TOON
parser. Both avoid silent mis-behavior on inputs that should never occur
in well-formed TOON but that fuzzers / callers with computed offsets can
still produce.

find_closing_quote:
  If `start >= content.len()`, the previous body would compute `start + 1`
  and enter a while loop whose body is never executed — benign today, but
  one arithmetic edit away from an overflow-adjacent bug. Short-circuit
  return None when start is past the end so the precondition is explicit.

find_unquoted_char:
  All callers pass ASCII targets (`:`, `|`, `\t`, `,`). The old body cast
  each byte with `bytes[i] as char`, which truncates UTF-8 continuation
  bytes to meaningless codepoints and could match a non-ASCII target
  character against a continuation byte of a multibyte scalar — a latent
  false-positive bug. New behavior:
    - Reject non-ASCII `target` up front (returns None).
    - Cache BACKSLASH / DOUBLE_QUOTE / target as bytes once.
    - Compare at the byte level so the scan stays strictly ASCII-safe
      and avoids the per-iteration `as char` cast.

No call-site behavior changes for current callers (all ASCII targets);
pathological inputs now return None deterministically instead of
potentially reading past the end or mis-matching on UTF-8 bytes.
